reasoning: The resolution criteria require the US federal government or California state government to mandate a license or permit to perform large AI training runs by the end of 2025. As of December 7, 2025, there is no such federal or Californian legislation or executive regulation that has been passed or enacted to impose an explicit licensing regime strictly for AI training runs. Prior California AI regulation (such as SB 1047) faced vetoes or did not contain a strict pre-training licensing hurdle, and recent laws taking effect in 2025 focus to date primarily on training data transparency and output disclosure (e.g., AB 2013). At the federal level, executive orders have required reporting and safety notifications for massive models crossing compute thresholds (e.g., 10^26 FLOPs) but explicitly stop short of a literal 'license or permit' to begin training. With only three weeks remaining in the year, and typical legislative calendars wrapping up, it is essentially impossible for a completely new, sweeping regulatory licensing regime to be proposed, passed, and enacted by December 31. The Metaculus community was at 0% in late November 2025, confirming the complete absence of any movement on this front. Thus, the probability is at the minimal bound of 0.02.
